user.title: Set up EcoFlow PowerOcean Plus
user.description:
Enter your EcoFlow account credentials. These are used exclusively to connect to the EcoFlow Cloud API.

**Note:** Use your EcoFlow App login credentials — not the Developer API Keys.

data_description.email: Email address of your EcoFlow user account
data_description.password: Password of your EcoFlow user account (stored encrypted)
data_description.serial_number: Device serial number (e.g. R37EXAMPLE000001) — found on the device label or in the EcoFlow App under device details.
data_description.num_battery_packs: Number of physically installed battery packs (1–9). Default for a 10 kWh system with two 5 kWh packs: 2.

error.invalid_auth: Incorrect email or password. Please use your EcoFlow App login credentials.
error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the EcoFlow API. Please check your internet connection.
error.invalid_serial: Invalid serial number. Please check the device label or the EcoFlow App.
error.missing_notification_target: Select a notification target or disable the daily sunset report.
error.unknown: Unexpected error. Please check the Home Assistant logs.

abort.already_configured: A device with this serial number is already configured.

data.power_outage_grid_power_threshold_w: Grid power threshold for outage detection (W)
data.power_outage_frequency_min_hz: Minimum grid frequency for valid grid signal (Hz)

data_description.num_battery_packs: Number of physically installed battery packs (1–9). The integration will reload automatically after saving.
data_description.debug_mode: Enables verbose logs for this integration. Reload the integration afterwards and download diagnostics if needed.
data_description.enable_daily_sunset_report: Sends a daily report at sunset with exported energy, calculated value, and battery-full duration.
data_description.daily_report_notify_target: Home Assistant notify entity that receives the daily report. It must be set when the report is enabled.
data_description.daily_report_feed_in_tariff_eur_per_kwh: Used to calculate the daily value. Example from the Westnetz billing data: 0.077 €/kWh.
data_description.enable_backup_helpers: Enables additional helper entities for backup runtime estimation and outage detection. The integration does not directly control third-party systems.
data_description.backup_reserved_soc_percent: Battery reserve that should remain untouched for backup planning. Example: 10 means the last 10% are excluded from the runtime estimate.
data_description.power_outage_grid_power_threshold_w: If the absolute grid power remains close to 0 W for a short period, this becomes one building block of the outage heuristic. Higher values make the detection more tolerant to measurement noise.
data_description.power_outage_frequency_min_hz: If grid frequency drops below this threshold or disappears after previously valid frequency samples, it is treated as evidence of a likely power outage. Keep this value clearly below 50 Hz.
data_description.backup_runtime_smoothing_minutes: Load power is averaged across this time window so single spikes do not distort the runtime estimate too much.
data_description.backup_critical_runtime_minutes: Below this estimated runtime the backup reserve is marked as critical. This is a useful trigger for your own automations.

error.missing_notification_target: Select a notification target or disable the daily sunset report.
